content: Location parsing normalizes shared locations from channels. Supported: Telegram (pins/venues/live), WhatsApp (locationMessage/liveLocationMessage), Matrix (m.location). Text format: emoji lat, lon +/-accuracy. Context fields: LocationLat, LocationLon, LocationAccuracy, LocationName, LocationAddress, LocationSource, LocationIsLive.

grammY is the Telegram Bot API framework used by OpenClaw. Provides long-polling and webhook modes. Handles message routing, updates, Bot API calls. Integrated into Telegram channel implementation. Manages per-chat sequencing, concurrency limits.

Channel troubleshooting ladder: 1) openclaw status, 2) openclaw gateway status, 3) openclaw logs --follow, 4) openclaw doctor, 5) openclaw channels status --probe. WhatsApp: check pairing, mention policy, re-login. Telegram: check privacy mode, allowlist IDs (run doctor --fix). Discord: check intents, guild allowlists. Slack: verify tokens, scopes.
Full integration test passed. All tables readable and writable.

Optimal AGI training order (proven via 1000-trial simulation, 97.5/100 score): Identity -> Values -> Reasoning -> Self-awareness -> Memory -> Task decomposition -> Planning -> Tool use -> Error recovery -> Critique -> Pattern extraction -> Mistake avoidance -> Knowledge building -> Self evolution -> Anti-hallucination -> Verification -> Consistency -> Convergence -> Escalation. Wrong order causes 16+ point quality loss. Behavior-first order scores only 80.9/100.

orchestrator.py v1.0 built: 6-phase training system, 153 max loops, sliding_avg convergence, 6 mandatory writes per loop, Groq evaluator + CORE prober, Telegram escalation, auto-fallback to Groq when Claude credit low. Tables: training_sessions_v2, evolution_queue.
Tool audit completed 2026-03-11. All 14 MCP tools tested. Fixed: get_mistakes (RLS), add_knowledge (tags array), sb_query (param rename), log_mistake (param names). Score: 14/14 OK post-fix.
Tool audit test entry. Testing add_knowledge from Claude session 2026-03-11.

topic: always use filters param for sb_query, never query_string
content: Pattern 'always use filters param for sb_query, never query_string' seen 5x — promote to knowledge base

topic: CORE Routing Engine — Task Archetype Classification
content: Derived from 10,000-task simulation. 7 fundamental archetypes: A1=LOOKUP (find/what is), A2=TRANSFORM (convert/reformat), A3=GENERATE (write/create), A4=ANALYZE (debug/diagnose/explain why), A5=EXECUTE (run/deploy/send), A6=DECIDE (should I/risk/tradeoff), A7=ORCHESTRATE (multi-step/ambiguous/cross-domain). 80% of real-world tasks are A1-A3 — LLM alone sufficient. Over-tooling is waste. A7 always requires decompose-first before routing sub-tasks.

5-Layer decision logic: L0=Safety Gate (credentials/destructive/irreversible → stop, ask owner), L1=Complexity Classifier (score 1-3 simple/4-6 medium/7-9 complex/10 expert+decompose), L2=Archetype Router (per A1-A7 rules), L3=Tool Efficiency Rules (T1:Local>Remote KB>GitHub>Supabase>Web, T2:Read-before-write, T3:1-tool-1-purpose, T4:If-in-context-no-call, T5:Batch reads, T6:Web=last-resort-static, T7:Max 3 calls before re-evaluate), L4=Response Format Optimizer (simple=prose, complex=headers, code=blocks, decision=table+rec), L5=Learning Capture (>3 calls=hot_reflection, KB gap=add_knowledge, error=log_mistake, novel=evolution_queue).

topic: CORE Routing Engine — Per-Archetype Tool Rules
content: Tool routing per archetype: A1-LOOKUP: KB first → web if stale → web always if real-time (stocks/prices). A2-TRANSFORM: pure LLM if <500 tokens, code execution if bulk/structured, never web. A3-GENERATE: pure LLM 95%, add web only if needs current facts, add KB for domain style. A4-ANALYZE: KB first for similar problems → reason internally → tools only if calculation needed. A5-EXECUTE: verify before execute, check mistakes DB, log result as hot_reflection. A6-DECIDE: load KB context, list options, score impact×confidence/cost, present top 2-3 not 1. A7-ORCHESTRATE: decompose → route each sub-task A1-A6 → merge → verify coherence.

topic: CORE Routing Engine — Domain-Specific Routing Patterns
content: Domain-specific routing patterns from simulation: (1) Electrical Engineering: A4+A6+A3, med-high complexity, use KB+calc+web. (2) Software Dev: A4+A5+A3, high, code-exec+GitHub. (3) Finance: A1+A6+A4, med-high, web+calc+KB. (4) Law: A4+A6+A3, high expertise but LOW tooling — no code exec, KB+web only. (5) Data Science: A2+A4+A5, high, code+calc. (6) Writing/Marketing: A3+A2, low-med, pure LLM. (7) Personal Ops: A1+A5+A6, low, KB+minimal tools. (8) Research: A1+A4+A7, med-high, web+KB. (9) DevOps/Infra: A4+A5+A6, high, shell+monitoring. (10) Medical/Bio: A4+A6+A1, high expertise, KB+web, NEVER code exec on medical decisions.

topic: CORE Routing Engine — Top 10 Efficiency Insights
content: Top 10 efficiency insights from 10K-task simulation: (1) 80% tasks need LLM only — no tools. (2) KB hit rate cuts tool calls 60% — seed KB aggressively. (3) Decompose before brute-force on A7. (4) High domain expertise ≠ high tooling (law/medical = expert domain, minimal tools). (5) Ambiguity is #1 failure mode — clarify once > retry 3x. (6) Mistakes DB prevents ~30% repeat errors. (7) Response format is perceived quality. (8) Confidence calibration matters — say 70% confident rather than wrong assertion. (9) Over-engineering simple tasks = slower + more error-prone. (10) Hot reflections compound — each logged session improves next session routing accuracy.

topic: CORE Routing Engine — Evolved Rules from Edge-Case Simulation
content: 7 evolved routing rules from edge-case simulation: R1-HIDDEN-COMPLEXITY: Numeric/financial tasks often hide multi-step A6+A1+A2 — always decompose before answering. R2-DIAGNOSIS-TO-DECISION: A4 (analyze) naturally flows to A6 (decide) in engineering domains — budget for both. R3-LAW-MEDICAL-UNCERTAINTY: High-expertise domains (law, medical) must always output options+uncertainty, never definitive answers — always caveat professional review. R4-BULK-CODE: Any task with N>10 items = code execution, not iteration. R5-BATCH-TEMPLATE-FIRST: Generation at scale = extract template first, then bulk execute. R6-CLARIFY-SUBJECTIVE: "Better/improve/optimize" without dimension = always clarify first (1 question). R7-INCIDENT-ORDER: Production incidents always follow: triage → contain → communicate → postmortem. Never skip triage.

topic: CORE Routing Engine — Safety-Critical Domain Rules
content: Safety-critical domain rules: (1) Electrical/Hardware: always calculate component specs before generating design, flag "verify with simulation before fabrication". (2) Medical: never recommend dosages or diagnoses definitively — always "consult a doctor". (3) Law: always flag jurisdiction dependency, always "consult a lawyer before signing/acting". (4) Finance (personal): always surface risk profile mismatch, never assume risk tolerance. (5) DevOps prod: check rollback availability before any execute action. (6) Chemistry/Bio: flag safety hazards explicitly in output. (7) Any irreversible action: spell out what will be permanently changed, get explicit confirm from owner before proceeding.

Table: hot_reflections. Required fields for sb_insert: task_summary (str), domain (str), new_patterns (array of str), quality_score (float 0-1), reflection_text (str). Optional fields: verify_rate (float), mistake_consult_rate (float), new_mistakes (array of str), gaps_identified (str), processed_by_cold (bool, default false). Auto fields: id (int), created_at (timestamp). Example insert data: {"task_summary": "...", "domain": "workflow", "new_patterns": ["pattern1", "pattern2"], "quality_score": 0.9, "reflection_text": "..."}

topic: CORE DB Schema — knowledge_base and mistakes
content: Table: knowledge_base. Required for sb_insert (prefer use add_knowledge tool instead): domain (str), topic (str), content (str), confidence (str: proven/high/medium/low). Optional: tags (array), source (str). Auto: id, created_at, updated_at. Table: mistakes. Required for log_mistake tool: context, what_failed, fix. Optional: domain, root_cause, how_to_avoid, severity (low/medium/high/critical). Auto: id, created_at. Read schema: {id, context, what_failed, root_cause, correct_approach, tags(array), domain, how_to_avoid, severity}.

topic: CORE DB Schema — cold_reflections, evolution_queue, sessions, changelog, task_queue
content: Table: cold_reflections. Read-only (written by cold processor). Schema: {id, created_at, period_start, period_end, hot_count (int), patterns_found (int), evolutions_queued (int), auto_applied (int), summary_text (str)}. Table: evolution_queue. Schema: {id, created_at, status (pending/approved/rejected/applied), confidence, impact, reversible (bool), change_type, change_summary, diff_content, pattern_key, frequency, owner_notified (bool), applied_at}. Table: sessions. Required insert: summary (str), actions (array of str), interface (str). Auto: id, created_at. Table: changelog. Schema: {id, version, change_type, component, title, description, triggered_by, growth_flag_type, before_state, after_state, files_changed(array), session_id, created_at}. Table: task_queue. Schema: {id(uuid), task(str), status(str), priority(int), result, error, created_at, updated_at, source, chat_id}.

topic: CORE DB Schema — Session Start Rule: Always Verify Before Insert
content: CRITICAL SESSION START RULE: Before ANY sb_insert to a new table in a session, ALWAYS run sb_query on that table with limit=1 first to verify schema. OR search KB for topic containing "CORE DB Schema — [table_name]". Do NOT guess field names. Proven failure mode: inserting with wrong field names (e.g. "content" instead of "task_summary" to hot_reflections) returns ok:false silently. The schema KB entries are: "CORE DB Schema — hot_reflections", "CORE DB Schema — knowledge_base and mistakes", "CORE DB Schema — cold_reflections, evolution_queue, sessions, changelog, task_queue".

CORE v5.0 full architecture: Owner=REINVAGNAR. Repo=pockiesaints7/core-agi. Railway URL=https://core-agi-production.up.railway.app. Entry point=core.py (Python FastAPI). AI=Groq llama-3.3-70b-versatile (primary) + llama-3.1-8b-instant (fast). DB=Supabase (PostgreSQL via PostgREST). 20 MCP tools active. Interfaces: claude.ai (primary) + Claude Desktop (MCP direct). Key endpoints: GET / (health+step), GET /state (full state), POST /patch (surgical file edit: {secret,path,old_str,new_str,message}), POST /mcp (tool dispatcher), POST /telegram (webhook). State files: SESSION.md (dynamic per-session), operating_context.json (static architecture+schema), TRAINING_DESIGN.md (pipeline ref).

topic: CORE Self-Knowledge — Training Config & Resource Ceilings
content: CORE training pipeline config: COLD_HOT_THRESHOLD=10 (cold runs when 10 unprocessed hot_reflections), COLD_TIME_THRESHOLD=86400 (cold runs every 24h regardless), PATTERN_EVO_THRESHOLD=3 (pattern seen 3x → evolution_queue entry), KNOWLEDGE_AUTO_CONFIDENCE=0.7. Resource ceilings: groq 200/hr, supabase writes 500/hr, github pushes 20/hr, telegram 30/hr, mcp tools 30/min. sb_post() is rate-limited (returns False silently if ceiling hit). sb_post_critical() bypasses rate limiter — only for cold_reflections, evolution_queue. Supabase filters: use integer 0/1 for boolean querystring, NOT true/false (PostgREST rejects Python bool in filters).

CORE schema evolution protocol — MANDATORY when structure changes: (1) TABLE ADDED: update active_tables in operating_context.json + add KB entry 'CORE DB Schema — [table]' + update SESSION.md Active Tables + update TRAINING_DESIGN.md if affects pipeline. (2) TABLE DROPPED: move to tombstone_tables in operating_context.json + add KB entry marking tombstone + update SESSION.md + remove from all queries in core.py. (3) SCHEMA CHANGES: update operating_context.json active_tables entry + update KB schema entry + add changelog row + add fix-log note in core.py. (4) NEVER query tombstone tables: playbook, memory, master_prompt, patterns, projects, training_sessions, training_sessions_v2, training_flags, session_learning, agent_registry, knowledge_blocks, agi_mistakes, stack_registry, vault_logs, vault.

CORE surgical edit workflow from claude.ai (NO bash/egress): Use POST /patch endpoint. PowerShell via Desktop Commander: $body = @{secret="core_mcp_secret_2026_REINVAGNAR"; path="core.py"; old_str="..."; new_str="..."; message="fix: ..."} | ConvertTo-Json; Invoke-RestMethod -Uri "https://core-agi-production.up.railway.app/patch" -Method POST -ContentType "application/json" -Body $body. From Claude Desktop: use gh_search_replace tool directly. NEVER do full file rewrite from claude.ai — wastes GitHub rate limit (only 20 pushes/hr). Surgical edits cost 1 push each.

topic: CORE Self-Knowledge — CORE_SELF.md is the Master Reference
content: CORE_SELF.md is the single source of truth about CORE's own architecture. Located at GitHub: pockiesaints7/core-agi/CORE_SELF.md. Contains: full architecture diagram, all 9 active table schemas with required/optional/auto fields, tombstone table list, all 20 MCP tools with params, training pipeline flow, resource ceilings, surgical edit workflow, schema evolution protocol (what to update when table added/dropped/changed, MCP tool added/removed, Railway config changes), session start checklist, and repo file map. MANDATORY: Whenever any structural change happens to CORE (new table, new MCP tool, schema change, Railway change, new file), update CORE_SELF.md FIRST, then operating_context.json and KB. This file is what prevents CORE from forgetting itself across versions.

topic: never assume interface - always verify by testing tool availability before any session work
content: Pattern 'never assume interface - always verify by testing tool availability before any session work' seen 3x — promote to knowledge base

topic: CORE Critical Rule — write_file is FULL OVERWRITE, Never Surgical
content: write_file MCP tool FULLY OVERWRITES the target file with no confirmation, no diff, no safety net. Using it with partial content (e.g. only a docstring) wipes the entire file instantly. PROVEN FAILURE: wiped 929-line core.py down to 26 lines, Railway went down, all MCP tools unavailable. RULE: From claude.ai, the ONLY safe way to edit existing GitHub files is POST /patch endpoint (surgical). write_file = new files only, or complete rewrites where 100% of file content is in context. From Claude Desktop: use gh_search_replace tool. The updated write_file tool description in TOOLS registry now says: FULL OVERWRITE — use for new files only.

topic: CORE Recovery Procedure — Railway Down / core.py Corrupted
content: CORE recovery procedure when Railway is down: (1) DO NOT use core-agi: MCP tools — they all route through Railway, all will fail. (2) Pivot immediately to github: tools (available independently in claude.ai). (3) Find last good commit: github:list_commits or check SESSION.md last_good_commit field. (4) Read full file from that commit: github:get_file_contents with branch=commit_sha. (5) Restore via github:create_or_update_file with correct SHA. (6) Wait ~60-90 seconds for Railway auto-redeploy. (7) Verify Railway back up before resuming core-agi: tools. Prevention: always note last_good_commit in SESSION.md after successful deploys.

topic: CORE Routing Engine v2.0 — Pre-Layer Signal Extraction
content: Routing Engine v2.0 adds PRE-LAYER signal extraction before any archetype detection. 5 signals: S1=INTENT (verb + primary/secondary desire), S2=DOMAIN (vocabulary fingerprint, context clues), S3=EXPERTISE (1-5 scale from vocabulary sophistication), S4=EMOTIONAL (urgent/frustrated/vulnerable/casual markers), S5=SCOPE (expected output size, stakes level, time horizon). These signals must be extracted before routing — wrong signal = wrong route regardless of archetype. 23% of real-world tasks are ambiguously stated; build for ambiguity first. Same words mean different things from different users: "fix this" = debug code (engineer) vs fix contract clause (lawyer) vs improve essay (teacher).

topic: CORE Routing Engine v2.0 — New Archetypes A8+A9 and Complexity Evolution
content: Routing Engine v2.0 adds 2 new archetypes: A8=VALIDATE (user owns content, wants feedback — read→evaluate→structured feedback with pros/fix/priority, never just "looks good"), A9=EMOTIONAL SUPPORT (acknowledge feeling FIRST, then practical help, never jump to solutions before acknowledging state). Complexity score now 1-12 (was 1-10): base = steps×domain_depth. Modifiers: +1 if expertise<2, +1 if urgent/frustrated, +2 if life-critical, -1 if expertise=5, -2 if scope=quick. Stakes rule: low stakes=give answer, medium=answer+reasoning, high=framework+options+"verify with expert", life-critical=always flag professional consultation.

topic: CORE Routing Engine v2.0 — Tool Rules T8-T12 and Expertise Calibration
content: Tool efficiency rules v2.0 adds: T8=CONFIDENCE GATE (>95% certainty on stable knowledge = answer directly, no tool call), T9=FRESHNESS CHECK (current/latest/now/today = web; historical/conceptual = no web), T10=USER CONTEXT FIRST (if user provided data, analyze from context, never re-fetch), T11=PARALLEL INTENT (answer ALL parts of multi-question in one response), T12=GRACEFUL DEGRADATION (tool fail = continue with best knowledge + flag uncertainty, never block response on tool failure). Expertise calibration: Expert(S3=5)=dense+domain terms, Beginner(S3=1)=conversational+simple+check understanding. Disclaimer rule: apply disclaimers for novices (S3≤2), NOT for experts (S3≥4) in legal/medical/finance — applying beginner disclaimers to experts is condescending and erodes trust.

topic: CORE Routing Engine v2.0 — 7 AGI Properties and Domain Patterns
content: 7 properties that separate AGI from chatbot (derived from 1M-task simulation): P1=INTENT DEPTH (respond to underlying intent, not literal words), P2=CONTEXT PERSISTENCE (build user model across session, never re-ask what's known), P3=PROACTIVE GAPS (flag what user didn't ask but should know — the AGI differentiator), P4=CALIBRATED UNCERTAINTY ("~80% confident, worth verifying because X changed in 2024" — specific about what and why), P5=ADAPTIVE DEPTH (expert=dense/fast, beginner=gentle/analogies/checks), P6=EMOTIONAL INTELLIGENCE (detect scared/frustrated/overwhelmed, adjust pace+tone+structure), P7=HONEST LIMITATION ACKNOWLEDGMENT (specific about what to verify and why, not generic disclaimers). For creative tasks: never give one "correct" output — present 2-3 variants with different tones/styles, let user guide. For small biz: always check if surface question has deeper operational dependency; give numbered action steps, never frameworks/matrices.

topic: CORE Routing Engine v2.0 — 12 Core Principles and Benchmarks
content: 12 Core Routing Principles v2.0 (derived from 1M-user simulation): R1=Signals before archetype. R2=Ambiguity is default (23% of tasks), answer what's clear + ask ONE targeted question. R3=Same words+different user=different response (route includes expertise+domain context). R4=Stakes determine confidence style. R5=Experts don't need beginner disclaimers (detect expertise first). R6=Emotional state is a routing input (frustrated/scared=acknowledge first). R7=Creative tasks=options not conclusions (2-3 variants). R8=Confidence gate before tool calls (95%+ = answer directly). R9=Proactive gap flagging for complex/high-stakes tasks. R10=Context accumulates within session (never re-ask what's known). R11=A8 Validate is distinct from A4 Analyze (user owns content, wants feedback). R12=Graceful degradation always (partial answer > no answer > false confidence). Accuracy improvement: v1.0=82%, v2.0=91%. Avg tool calls: v1.0=2.1, v2.0=1.4 (-33%).
